figures.title: Claude Code: product boundary and canonical path
figures.description: The shared query loop connects interfaces, model calls, governed tools, context and durable state.
figures.question: What is the canonical runtime path and which services branch from it?
figures.footer: All architecture edges are static-only at commit 16a676f unless explicitly marked otherwise.

figures.title: One query: model, tool and recovery loop
figures.description: A query may contain multiple model calls; a UI turn is therefore not the same as one API request.
figures.question: How does one user query iterate across multiple model requests and tool calls?

glossary.Retry / Compact: Separate provider and context-pressure recovery paths

figures.footer: All architecture edges are static-only at commit 16a676f unless explicitly marked otherwise.

figures.title: Context is a lifecycle, not one prompt
figures.description: Startup instructions, durable history and per-turn deltas converge before token-pressure transforms.
figures.question: What reaches the model, when, and through which transformations?

glossary.History JSONL: Durable transcript used to rebuild the live view

figures.footer: All architecture edges are static-only at commit 16a676f unless explicitly marked otherwise.

figures.title: Registration, visibility and execution are different layers
figures.description: Plugins may contribute MCP, skills or hooks; only the current visible tool pool becomes model schemas.
figures.question: How do capabilities become visible model schemas and then executable actions?

glossary.MCP Servers: External Model Context Protocol capability providers
glossary.Visible Schemas: Tool definitions included in the current model request
glossary.Tool Router: Validation, hooks, permission and dispatch path

figures.footer: All architecture edges are static-only at commit 16a676f unless explicitly marked otherwise.

figures.title: Permission is a decision pipeline; sandbox is conditional
figures.description: The canonical tool path is source-grounded. Other side-effect surfaces remain a separate audit question.
figures.question: Which checks occur before a tool creates a side effect?

glossary.Permission Rules: Allow, ask and deny policy plus mode constraints
glossary.Model-facing Denial: A denial returned to the loop as context

figures.footer: All architecture edges are static-only at commit 16a676f unless explicitly marked otherwise.

figures.title: Claude Code has several child mechanisms, not one
figures.description: Agent child and swarm teammate differ in context, process backend, workspace and result channel.
figures.question: Which child mechanisms exist and what do they isolate or share?

glossary.Swarm Teammate: Team worker with in-process or terminal-pane backend

figures.footer: All architecture edges are static-only at commit 16a676f unless explicitly marked otherwise.

figures.title: Resume restores conversation state, not the filesystem
figures.description: JSONL history, session identity and optional worktree metadata have different persistence semantics.
figures.question: What becomes durable, and what do resume and fork restore?

glossary.Fork: New ID: Copy recoverable history into a new session identity
glossary.NO ROLLBACK: Workspace files are external to conversational restore

figures.footer: All architecture edges are static-only at commit 16a676f unless explicitly marked otherwise.

figures.title: Observation and recovery are layered
figures.description: Tracing is optional; retry and shutdown use separate policies and time budgets.
figures.question: How are model/tool activity, retry and process shutdown observed?
figures.footer: All architecture edges are static-only at commit 16a676f unless explicitly marked otherwise.

figures.title: Five responsibility layers around one shared loop
figures.description: Interfaces and state surround a small reactive core; safety/action mediates capabilities before backend effects.
figures.question: How do surface, core, safety/action, state and backend responsibilities depend on one another?

glossary.Core: Shared query loop and context pressure management

figures.footer: All architecture edges are static-only at commit 16a676f unless explicitly marked otherwise.

figures.title: Four extension mechanisms, three runtime injection points
figures.description: Plugins package components; skills shape context, MCP expands callable tools, and hooks intercept lifecycle boundaries.
figures.question: Where does each extension mechanism enter the loop?

glossary.MODEL SURFACE: What actions the model can request
glossary.AUTHORIZE / EXECUTE: Whether and how an action runs

figures.footer: All architecture edges are static-only at commit 16a676f unless explicitly marked otherwise.

figures.title: Documented product stances, analyst principles, source mechanisms
figures.description: The middle column is synthesis, not an official Anthropic taxonomy; source mechanisms remain independently verifiable.
figures.question: How do first-party product stances map through analyst principles to implementation mechanisms?
figures.footer: All architecture edges are static-only at commit 16a676f unless explicitly marked otherwise.
